PIRAMAL PHARMA – SEGMENT-WISE BUSINESS UPDATE (Q4 FY26)

CDMO BUSINESS
• Revenue ₹1,708 Cr (↓ 4% YoY) | FY26 ↓ 10% YoY (destocking impact)  
• Adjusted growth positive excluding destocking  
• Order inflows improved post Oct’25 (US biopharma recovery)  
• Strong demand in ADCs, HP APIs, injectables  

• Capex ~$90 Mn for Lexington, Riverview expansion  
• Pipeline: 155+ molecules, 25 in Phase-3  
• Customer audits 209 (↑ 27% YoY)  
• Regulatory: Zero OAI; 38 inspections incl. 3 USFDA  

• FY27 drivers: order recovery, capacity ramp-up, cost optimisation  

COMPLEX HOSPITAL GENERICS (CHG)  
• Revenue ₹755 Cr (↑ 7% YoY) | FY26 ↑ 3% YoY  
• #1 US Sevoflurane with 47% share  
• #1 US Intrathecal Baclofen  

• Ex-US anesthesia impacted; recovery expected FY27  
• Injectable pain portfolio facing supply constraints  

• Kenalog® acquired ($35 Mn upfront + $65 Mn contingent)  
• Expands presence US, Europe, APAC with limited competition  

• FY27 focus: Kenalog integration, anesthesia recovery, cost control  

CONSUMER HEALTHCARE (PCH)  
• Revenue ₹320 Cr (↑ 17% YoY) | FY26 ₹1,274 Cr (↑ 17% YoY)  
• Outperforming industry growth  

• Power brands 52% contribution (↑ from 42% FY23)  
• E-commerce 27% contribution (↑ from 16% FY23)  

• 31 new premium products launched in FY26  
• ~12% sales reinvested in branding, promotions  
• Distribution: 180,000+ chemists, 13,000+ MT, 20+ e-commerce platforms  

• FY27 drivers: premiumisation, e-commerce, distribution expansion  

OVERALL VIEW  
• CDMO near-term impacted but recovery visible  
• CHG stable with leadership positions  
• Consumer healthcare strong growth engine  

Key Takeaway
• Transition year behind; multi-engine growth set for FY27  

Impact: Positive – recovery + diversification + margin improvement ahead

ONESOURCE SPECIALTY PHARMA RECEIVES EU GMP CERTIFICATE 🇪🇺💊✅

• 🏭 Sterile Product Division (SPD) facility in Bangalore receives EU GMP Certificate of Compliance

• 🇩🇪 Certificate issued by German authority — State Office for Occupational Safety, Social Affairs & Health of Schleswig-Holstein

• 📅 Certificate dated April 27, 2026; based on inspection conducted on October 14, 2025

• 🧪 Covers sterile products including aseptically prepared small volume liquids, secondary packing & QC testing

• 💉 SPD capabilities: Liquid vials, lyophilised vials, pre-filled syringes & autoinjectors

• 🌍 Facility already holds approvals from US-FDA, Health Canada, ANVISA & TGA

🟢 Impact: Positive (EU GMP certification opens European market access + strengthens global regulatory credibility for sterile products)

NAMO EWASTE – STRUCTURAL LONG-TERM STORY

• Policy + capacity expansion driving multi-year opportunity  
• Not just recycling → ecosystem-level transformation  

EPR = STRUCTURAL DEMAND
• Producers mandated to recycle fixed % of waste  
• Non-compliance → buy certificates from recyclers  
• Creates recurring, non-cyclical demand  
• Potential pricing power over time  

SUPPLY FORMALIZATION
• Shift from informal → organized sector  
• CPCB registration, digital tracking, compliance norms  
• Higher utilisation for organized players  
• Industry consolidation likely  

RAW MATERIAL VISIBILITY
• Producer-led collection improves supply predictability  
• Reduced dependence on fragmented sourcing  
• Better operating leverage potential  

CAPACITY EXPANSION ROADMAP
• Nashik Li-ion recycling (FY26)  
• Palwal consolidation (efficiency, scale)  
• Hyderabad plant (FY27 ramp-up)  
• Hydromet refining (FY27–29 value addition)  

MARGIN EXPANSION DRIVER
• Current: collection + shredding (low margin)  
• Future: refining Li, Co, Ni, Mn (high margin)  
• EPR credits add incremental revenue  
• Guided gross margins ~18–20% (battery recycling)  

EV WASTE MEGATREND
• EV adoption rising → battery scrap growth ahead  
• Domestic

MSUMI Q4 FY26 – PERFORMANCE ANALYSIS

• Revenue ₹3,334.62 Cr (↑ 32.9% YoY), outperforming PV industry  
• EBITDA margin 8.22% (↓ 259 bps YoY) due to raw material pressure  
• Material cost ↑ 47% YoY, led by copper inflation  

CASH FLOW & BALANCE SHEET  
• Operating cash flow ₹761.32 Cr (↑ YoY), CFO/PAT 1.22x  
• Free cash flow ₹559.95 Cr (strong generation)  
• Equity ₹2,161.71 Cr (↑ 27.3% YoY, bonus impact)  
• Virtually debt-free; borrowings ~₹10.40 Cr  

EFFICIENCY METRICS  
• ROCE 35.3% vs 42.6% YoY (miss vs >40% target)  
• Working capital ₹1,631.55 Cr (↑ 21.1% YoY)  

BUSINESS DYNAMICS  
• Strong topline growth across segments  
• Greenfield plants still ramping up  
• Profitability expected in next 2–3 quarters  
• Operating leverage to kick in with utilisation  

Key Positives  
• Strong revenue growth vs industry  
• Robust cash generation, FCF strength  
• Clean balance sheet, low leverage  

Key Risks  
• High commodity costs (copper) impacting margins  
• ROCE dilution vs target levels  
• Rising working capital requirements  
• Delay in greenfield profitability  

Key Takeaway  
• Strong

In India, cable demand of approximately ₹35,000 crore is expected solely from data centers over the next five years.

In the total project cost of a data center, the share of cables and wires is approximately 3% to 4%.

For every 1 GW of data center capacity, the cabling cost will be around ₹3,500 crore to ₹4,000 crore.

By FY31, India is expected to add 8–10 GW of new data center capacity. To meet this 8–10 GW capacity, the total cable demand in the Indian market could reach approximately ₹28,000 crore to ₹35,000 crore.

Source : Apar Industries Q3FY26 Earnings report

JET FUEL MARKET – MORGAN STANLEY VIEW

SUPPLY DISRUPTION (NW EUROPE)
• Jet fuel imports fall to record lows
• Loadings <200,000 bpd; total ~2 million bpd
• ~300,000 bpd Middle East supply gap persists

SUPPLY CONSTRAINTS
• EU refineries operating at full capacity
• Africa + US add only 100–150k bpd
• Net shortfall ~150–200k bpd remains

MARGIN OUTLOOK
• March arrivals offer temporary relief
• Structural tightness keeps crack margins elevated

AIRLINE RESPONSE
• Ryanair maintains capacity
• Wizz Air, TUI Group cut capacity
• Lufthansa cuts most aggressively

Key Takeaway
• Supply deficit driving sustained high jet fuel margins

Impact: Mixed – positive for refiners, negative for airlines

UAE ENERGY MINISTER – OIL MARKET COMMENTARY

• Suhail Al Mazrouei indicates decision taken independently without direct consultation with other producers

• Highlights concern over rapid depletion of global strategic crude reserves
• Suggests current environment is “unprecedented” from a supply-security perspective

• Emphasizes need to prioritize consumer-side stability in energy markets
• Signals flexibility in decision-making given no binding obligation under group dynamics

• Implies proactive stance to ensure supply availability amid global uncertainty

Impact: Mixed to Positive – comments indicate potential for increased supply flexibility (cooling oil prices), but also highlight underlying stress in global energy reserves, keeping volatility elevated

---

📍 NEW PROJECTS

RELIANCE INDUSTRIES: To invest ₹1.6 lakh Cr in India’s largest 1.5 GW AI data centre cluster in Andhra Pradesh.

MEGHNA INFRACON INFRASTRUCTURE: Adds 5 redevelopment projects worth ₹600 Cr GDV, crossing ₹1,000 Cr milestone.

---

📍 Q2 / BUSINESS UPDATE

INOX GREEN ENERGY : NCLT AHMEDABAD APPROVES DEMERGER OF POWER EVACUATION BUSINESS INTO INOX RENEWABLE SOLUTIONS ⚡🏗️

• ⚖️ Hon'ble NCLT Ahmedabad Bench pronounced order on March 13, 2026 | Certified copy received by IGESL on April 27, 2026 | Scheme sanctioned under Sections 230-232 of Companies Act, 2013

• 🔄 Power Evacuation Business (Demerged Undertaking) of Inox Green Energy Services Limited (IGESL) to be transferred and vested in Inox Renewable Solutions Limited (IRSL) | Appointed Date: October 1, 2024 | Scheme effective from date of filing certified copy with RoC, Gujarat

• 🎯 Strategic rationale: IGESL to emerge as a pure-play Wind O&M company | IRSL to consolidate Power Evacuation + EPC business — enabling focused growth and distinct investor profiles for both entities

• 📊 Share entitlement ratio: 122 equity shares of IRSL (FV ₹10) for every 1,000 equity shares of IGESL (FV ₹10) | Scheme approved unanimously by all classes of shareholders, warrant holders, secured & unsecured creditors | No statutory authority opposed the scheme

🟢 Impact: Positive (NCLT approval is a significant structural milestone — pure-play O&M demerger unlocks focused valuation for both IGESL and IRSL; separate listing of IRSL creates additional value discovery opportunity for existing shareholders)

Adani Wilmar: Edible Oil business recorded a volume growth of 17% YoY in Q4 FY26

At the time when there was a gas ⛽️ shortage
Chemicals Sector in India , Capex tracker , Deepak Nitrite is doing the largest capex in the sector ( FY25-FY28)

UAE exits OPEC from May 2026 🛢️

Big signal: more flexibility in oil production decisions

What UAE said:
• Will focus on national interest & market stability
• Continue investing across oil, gas & renewables
• Aim to balance supply-demand, not restrict it
👉 What it means for India 🇮🇳
• Positive bias for oil supply → reduces risk of sharp spikes
• India already less vulnerable (oil imports ↓ as % of GDP)
• Lower volatility = better control on inflation & fiscal
• Supports macros: CAD, rupee stability & growth outlook
But key variable remains ⏳
➡️ Geopolitics + supply disruptions

If flows stay stable → oil spikes likely temporary
If disruption worsens → short-term pressure

👉 Net takeaway:
UAE exit = more supply-side flexibility
For India, that’s structurally more cushion than risk 📉

reports.text:
**JAIN RESOURCE RECYCLING (JAINREC) – THE "URBAN MINING" COMPOUNDER**

**STRATEGIC PIVOT: FORWARD INTEGRATION IN COPPER**
• **Current Status:** Transitioning from primary recycling (Scrap to Ingots) to high-value-added products.
• **Phased Commissioning (Unit 3):**
  - **Copper Anodes:** Started Feb 2026 (800 MT/month); doubling to 1,600 MT in Q1 FY27.
  - **Copper Cathodes:** Started Mar 2026 (750 MT/month); doubling to 1,500 MT by Q3 FY27.
  - **Downstream:** Wire rods (600 MT) and Busbars (500 MT) expected in Q1 FY27.
• **Margin Impact:** Shift to value-added products is expected to push EBITDA/MT above ₹55,000 (from ~₹46,000 baseline).

**JOINT VENTURES & RAW MATERIAL SECURITY**
• **Jain CY Circular Solutions (Ahmedabad):** JV with US-based C&Y Group to process 72,000 MT of scrap per annum.
  - **Output:** ~25,000 MTPA of processed copper starting June 2026.
  - **Benefit:** Direct access to C&Y’s 11-12 US-based scrap yards, securing high-quality feedstock.
• **Kuwait Expansion:** Acquired 25% stake in a battery recycling plant (2,000 MT/month) to secure Middle East scrap flows, operational by Q3 FY27.

**MULTI-METAL DIVERSIFICATION & "GREENIUM"**
• **Critical Metals:** Tin capacity doubled to 500 MTPA; Antimony extraction (100 MT/month) from lead bullion slated for Q3 FY27.
• **Green Advantage:** "Zero waste, zero burning" philosophy using green fuels allows the company to target premium "Green Metal" procurement contracts from global OEMs like Apple and Siemens.
• **Market Positioning:** One of the few multi-commodity recyclers (Lead, Copper, Aluminum, Tin, Antimony) globally, reducing dependence on any single metal cycle.

**FINANCIAL SNAPSHOT (9M FY26)**
• **Revenue:** ₹6,438 Cr (↑ 38% YoY).
• **EBITDA:** ₹449 Cr (↑ 65% YoY) | Margin: 7.0%.
• **PAT:** ₹281 Cr (↑ 65% YoY).
• **Return Ratios:** ROE at 30.1% | ROCE at 24.9% (Pre-expansion metrics).
• **Valuation:** Trading at ~40-60x P/E, reflecting a significant premium over peers like Gravita and Pondy Oxides due to its aggressive 40-50% growth guidance.

**CORE INSIGHT**
• JRR is effectively an **"L&T of Recycling,"** managing four massive concurrent expansion projects.
• The strategic shift into **Data Center-grade copper products** (Busbars/Anodes) aligns perfectly with the AI infrastructure boom you’ve been tracking.
• The main monitorable is the **Working Capital Cycle** (currently ~82 days); management expects a drawdown to 60-65 days as the new value-added units stabilize.

**Impact: Highly Positive (Execution-led Re-rating)**

SKIPPER LTD – Q4 & FY26 PERFORMANCE SUMMARY

BIG PICTURE: RECORD-BREAKING FY26

• Net Profit (PAT): ₹2,073 Mn (Highest ever | ↑ 42% YoY).
• Order Inflow: ₹56,780 Mn (All-time high).

Q4 FY26 FINANCIAL SNAPSHOT

• EBITDA: ₹1,734 Mn (↑ 40.2% YoY).
• EBITDA Margin: 10.4% (↑ 80 bps YoY).
• PAT: ₹756 Mn (↑ 70.3% YoY).
• Efficiency: Finance costs reduced to 3.3% of revenue (vs 4.4% YoY).

SEGMENTAL PERFORMANCE
• Engineering (79% Mix): Revenue ↑ 24% YoY; EBITDA margins at ~11.7%.
• Polymer (9% Mix): Revenue ↑ 17% YoY; Volume growth at 20%.
• Infrastructure (12% Mix): Focus on margin improvement over volume.
• Exports: Hit by West Asia geopolitics (down 70% YoY); being offset by domestic 800kV HVDC and 765kV project wins.

CAPACITY & INNOVATION
• Expansion: Engineering capacity growing from 375k MTPA to 450k MTPA by June 2026.
• R&D Moat: World-first "Dual Tower Test Bed" operational; testing towers up to 1200 kV.
• Digital: SAP S4 HANA implementation completed for better operational control.

INDUSTRY TAILWINDS (THE MULTI-DECADE THEME)
• Domestic: ₹7.9 Lakh Cr transmission investment planned in India.
• Global: High demand for electrification, EV infra, and renewable integration.
• Positioning: Top 10 global T&D manufacturer; major beneficiary of "China+1" strategy.

CORE INSIGHT
• Skipper is at a structural inflection point, shifting from a "Tower Manufacturer" to a "High-Value EPC & Innovation" player.
• The record order book and massive ₹9 Trillion NEP pipeline provide multi-year growth visibility.
• While working capital (debtor days) saw a stretch this quarter, the sharp rise in ROE (14.1%) and EBITDA margins suggests strong operating leverage is kicking in.

Impact: Highly Positive

• Operating Profit: ₹162.80 Cr (↑ 24.0% YoY).

ASSET QUALITY & TRENDS
• GNPA / NNPA: 1.9% / 1.3% (Stable and improving QoQ).
• 1+ DPD: 6.0% (Improved from 7.3% YoY).
• 30+ DPD: 3.7% (Improved from 4.7% YoY).
• Credit Cost: ₹27.9 Cr (↓ 14.3% YoY) — Reflecting high-quality secured growth.

NETWORK & VALUATION
• Expansion: 34 new branches in Q4 (148 total for FY26).
• Co-location: 70 co-located branches with Federal Bank.
• Book Value: ₹78.20 per share (↑ 14.4% YoY).
• Valuation: Currently trading at ~1.94x P/B.

CORE INSIGHT
• FedFina has effectively transformed into a "Gold Loan heavy" NBFC, which has significantly de-risked the balance sheet (98.9% secured) and lowered credit costs.
• The 76% surge in Gold AUM is a record for the company, suggesting they are aggressively gaining market share from unorganized lenders and larger peers.
• The ROAA of 2.6% puts them in the top-tier of mid-sized NBFCs, and with the current branch expansion, the operating leverage is expected to play out strongly in FY27.

Impact: Highly Positive

BANDHAN BANK – Q4 FY26 PERFORMANCE SUMMARY

ASSET QUALITY & RECOVERY (KEY HIGHLIGHTS)
• GNPA: 3.27% (Significant improvement vs 4.71% YoY).
• NNPA: 0.97% (Below the 1% threshold).
• Provisions: ₹(Dropped 46.3% YoY) as legacy stress subsides.
• Credit Cost: 2% (Improved by 195 bps YoY).
• Collection Efficiency (EEB): 99.3% (Strong recovery in micro-credit).

LOAN BOOK & DEPOSITS
• Advances: ₹(↑ 12.6% YoY) driven by Retail (ex-housing) at +46% YoY.
• Secured Mix: 56.2% of total book (Strategic shift toward lower risk).
• Deposits: ₹(↑ 10.0% YoY).
• CASA Ratio: 29.3% (Lower YoY but improved from 27.3% QoQ).

PROFITABILITY METRICS
• Net Profit (PAT): ₹(↑ 68.0% YoY | ↑ 159.2% QoQ) due to lower credit costs.
• NIM: 6.2% (Compressed from 6.7% YoY but improved QoQ).
• ROA / ROE: 1.1% / 8.5% (Marked turnaround from previous lows).
• NII: ₹(↑ 1.4% YoY) reflecting a cautious yield environment.

CORE INSIGHT
• Bandhan Bank is successfully transitioning from a volatile micro-lender to a diversified retail bank, with the secured book now over 56%.
• The sharp drop in credit costs and GNPA suggests the worst of the post-pandemic asset quality stress is finally in the rearview mirror.
• While the CASA ratio remains a monitorable, the sequential jump in ROA (from 0.4% to 1.1%) indicates a return to structural profitability.

Impact: Positive (Turnaround Play)

QUICK COMMERCE (BLINKIT – THE GROWTH ENGINE)
• NOV: ₹14,386 Cr (↑ 8.2% QoQ | ↑ 95.4% YoY).
• EBITDA: ₹37 Cr (0.3% of NOV) — Consistent Positivity.
• Economics: Gross Profit at 26.9% | Contribution Margin at 5.4%.
• Scale: 2,243 stores | 273.9M orders | ₹525 Avg. Order Value (NAOV).
• Velocity: NOV per store per day at ₹768k.

GOING-OUT & B2B (HYPERPURE)
• Going-Out NOV: ₹2,736 Cr (↑ 46.5% YoY).

CONSOLIDATED SUMMARY

Impact: Highly Positive

GREENPLY INDUSTRIES : INVESTS ₹15 CR IN JV GREENPLY SAMET (GSPL) + CEO RESIGNATION 🪵⚙️

• 💰 Infusing up to ₹15 crore into Greenply Samet Private Limited (GSPL) via equity share subscription for CAPEX & working capital — Greenply will continue to hold 50% stake post transaction

• 🏭 GSPL is a JV between Greenply Industries & Samet B.V. — manufactures & sells functional furniture hardware (drawer slide systems, hinge systems, lift-up systems, connection fittings) through a manufacturing facility in India

• 📊 GSPL financials: FY26 Total Income ₹44.27 Cr | PAT -₹50.47 Cr | Net Worth ₹97.87 Cr (revenue scaling but still loss-making)

• 📅 Acquisition in one or more tranches during FY2026-27 | Cash consideration | Related party transaction at arm's length

• 👤 Mr. Manoj Tulsian has resigned as Joint Managing Director & CEO of the Company

🟡 Impact: Mixed (₹15 Cr infusion into a still loss-making JV is a cautious move — hardware business has strong long-term potential as Greenply diversifies beyond plywood/laminates; CEO resignation adds management uncertainty and warrants monitoring)

TATA SONS: RBI REAFFIRMS 'UPPER LAYER' STATUS AMID LISTING DEBATE

• RBI continues classifying Tata Sons as Upper-Layer NBFC (CIC).
• Latest NBFC list released April 10, 2026, retains classification.
• Voluntary surrender of CIC registration remains under RBI review.
• New draft norms propose ₹1 lakh crore asset threshold.
• Tata Sons assets (~₹1.75 lakh crore) exceed proposed threshold.
• Upper-layer status mandates listing on stock exchanges.
• Internal debate continues between Tata Trusts and holding leadership.
• SP Group reportedly urging RBI to enforce mandatory listing.

Impact: Neutral/Watch — Ongoing regulatory limbo keeps listing pressure high for group holding companies.

reports.text:
ETERNAL LIMITED – QUICK COMMERCE (BLINKIT) DEEP DIVE

GROWTH TRAJECTORY
• Historical: 104% CAGR (FY23–FY26); growth moderating on larger base.
• Outlook: Management targets ~60%+ CAGR for next 3 years.
• Scale-up: Implies >4x growth from current levels.
• Market: Under-penetrated; focus remains on 15–20 core cities.

COMPETITION & SEASONALITY
• Competition: Short-term discounting pressure vs. long-term market expansion.
• Seasonality: Q4 is seasonally weakest; fewer days vs Q3.
• QoQ Performance: 8% growth; reflects post-festive dip in high-value items.
• Recovery: Q1 FY27 expected to rebound strongly.

PROFITABILITY MILESTONES (EBITDA MARGINS)
• FY23: –17.8%
• FY24: –3.7%
• FY25: –1.3%
• FY26: –0.6%
• Q4 FY26: +0.3% (Break-even achieved).

MARGIN GUIDANCE
• Mature Markets: Delhi NCR nearing 5–6% steady-state margins.
• Long-term Goal: Management confident in 5–6% aggregate margins.
• Philosophy: Structural profitability; growth remains the primary priority.

CORE INSIGHT
• Achieving EBITDA break-even in Q4 FY26 is the most critical de-risking event for the Blinkit thesis.
• The pivot from "cash burn" to "self-sustaining growth" allows management to combat aggressive discounting by new entrants without depleting the ~₹18,000 Cr cash reserve.
• The >4x scale-up target suggests that the "Quick Commerce" model is effectively cannibalizing traditional e-commerce and local kiranas faster than anticipated.

Impact: Highly Positive

ETERNAL (ZOMATO): Q4FY26 CONSOLIDATED GROWTH

• NOV hit ₹26,880 crore, up 54% YoY.
• Revenue surged 186% to ₹17,680 crore.
• Adjusted EBITDA grew 160% to ₹429 crore.
• Cash reserves remain high at ₹17,972 crore.
• Food Delivery EBITDA margin reached 5.5%.
• Blinkit NOV nearly doubled to ₹14,386 crore.
• Quick Commerce revenue skyrocketed 674% YoY.
• Hyperpure B2B business achieved ₹5 crore EBITDA.

Impact: Positive – Dominant growth with sustained profitability.

Zepto using the same template coming for IPO in next 3 months guiding for first PAT by FY29.

ETERNAL LIMITED – Q4 FY26 PERFORMANCE SUMMARY

CONSOLIDATED FINANCIALS
• B2C GOV (NOV): ₹26,880 Cr (↑ 54% YoY).
• Adjusted Revenue: ₹17,680 Cr (↑ 186% YoY).
• Adjusted EBITDA: ₹429 Cr (↑ 160% YoY).
• Cash Balance: ₹17,972 Cr (↑ ₹152 Cr QoQ).

FOOD DELIVERY SEGMENT
• GOV (NOV): ₹9,757 Cr (↑ 19% YoY).
• Adjusted Revenue: ₹3,125 Cr (↑ 30% YoY).
• Adjusted EBITDA: ₹532 Cr (5.5% margin).
• Growth: Steady baseline with improved profitability.

QUICK COMMERCE (BLINKIT)
• GOV (NOV): ₹14,386 Cr (↑ 95% YoY).
• Adjusted Revenue: ₹13,232 Cr (↑ 674% YoY).
• Adjusted EBITDA: ₹37 Cr (Turned positive).
• Milestone: First full quarter of EBITDA positivity.

B2B SUPPLIES (HYPERPURE)
• Adjusted Revenue: ₹978 Cr (↑ 37% LFL).
• Adjusted EBITDA: ₹5 Cr (0.5% margin).
• Status: Operational turnaround with positive EBITDA.

GOING-OUT SEGMENT
• GOV (NOV): ₹2,736 Cr (↑ 47% YoY).
• Adjusted Revenue: ₹277 Cr (↑ 21% YoY).
• Adjusted EBITDA: –₹81 Cr (Investments in District app).
• Focus: Aggressive scaling of the new vertical.

CORE INSIGHT
• Quick Commerce GOV has officially surpassed Food Delivery GOV.
• EBITDA positivity in Blinkit is a massive structural de-risking.
• Massive cash pile of ~₹18,000 Cr provides huge M&A optionality.

Impact: Highly Positive

DALMIA BHARAT – Q4 FY26 & FY26 PERFORMANCE

Q4 FY26 PERFORMANCE
• Volume: 8.80 MnT (↑ 3% YoY)
• Revenue: ₹4,245 Cr (↑ 3.8% YoY)
• EBITDA: ₹902 Cr (↑ 13.7% YoY)
• PAT: ₹394 Cr (↓ 10.3% YoY)
• EBITDA/Tonne: ₹1,023 (↑ 10.4% YoY)

FY26 FULL YEAR HIGHLIGHTS
• Sales Volume: 30 MnT (↑ 2.2% YoY)
• Revenue: ₹14,804 Cr (↑ 5.9% YoY)
• EBITDA: ₹3,083 Cr (↑ 28.1% YoY)
• PAT: ₹1,157 Cr (↑ 65.5% YoY)
• EBITDA/Tonne: ₹1,027 (↑ 25.3% YoY)

FINANCIAL STRENGTH & RETURNS
• Leverage: Net Debt/EBITDA at 0.46x.
• Dividend: Final Dividend of ₹5/share.
• Efficiency: Strong ROCE focus via cost optimization.
• Liquidity: Significant capacity to fund ongoing expansion.

OPERATIONAL & STRATEGIC GROWTH
• Drivers: Better realizations and premium product mix.
• Trade Share: Improved quality of sales via trade.
• Energy: Renewable capacity increased to 449 MW.
• Additions: 15MW WHRS and 24MW renewable commissioned.

MANAGEMENT OUTLOOK
• Infra growth driving long-term demand tailwinds.
• Disciplined, balance-sheet-led capacity expansion.
• Price improvements offsetting geopolitical cost pressures.
• Strategic roadmap remains focused on ROCE.

Impact: Positive

• Sustenance sales to reach 75% of mix.
• New launch reliance drops to 25% share.
• FY27 focus on Mahalaxmi and Mahalunge launches.
• Aggressive Mumbai redevelopment expansion planned through FY30.
• High-growth pipeline includes Chembur and Matunga projects.
• Strategy prioritizes Mumbai and Pune micro-market dominance.

Impact: Positive – High growth visibility via premium inventory.

reports.text:
LT FOODS (DAAWAT): KIDZANIA IMMERSIVE FACTORY LAUNCH

• Launched "DAAWAT Immersive Learning Factory" at KidZania India on April 28, 2026
• Targets kids (4–14 years) through experiential "Farm-to-Fork" basmati journey
• Role-play activities include Production Engineer, processing, and quality checks
• Duration: 25-minute interactive session covering the full rice production lifecycle
• Takeaways: Digital certificate and 200g DAAWAT rice pack for participants

Congratulations on the successful new oil & gas discovery to Oil India Limited and Indian Oil Corporation Limited (the Indian Consortium partners with SIPEX, Algeria as the operator) in the contract area 95/96 in Ghadames Basin, Libya. 

The well was completed to a final depth of 8,440 feet and achieved production of 13 million cubic feet of gas and 327 barrels of condensate per day during testing from the Awynat Wanin and Awyn Kaza formations.

The discovery reflects the growing global footprints of Indian energy companies, importance of strategic international alliances, and our commitment to strengthening national energy security through overseas assets acquisition by national oil companies. 

Wishing the consortium continued success in unlocking greater value from this promising asset.

**BHARTI AIRTEL (NXTRA) – GOOGLE AI HUB PROJECT**

**PROJECT OVERVIEW: THE 1GW HUB**
• **Location:** Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh.
• **Scale:** Groundbreaking for India’s first gigawatt-scale AI Hub.
• **Investment:** A cornerstone of Google’s $15 Billion "AI-First India" commitment (2026–2030).
• **Significance:** This is not just a data center; it is a specialized AI compute cluster designed for high-density GPU workloads.

**AIRTEL’S CRITICAL ROLE (NXTRA + ADANICONNEX)**
• **Partnership:** Nxtra by Airtel is collaborating with AdaniConneX to co-develop the campus.
• **Responsibility:** Airtel handles the "Data Center Lifecycle"—from construction and power management to operational connectivity.
• **Infrastructure Lock-in:** Solidifies Airtel’s position as the preferred infrastructure partner for global hyperscalers entering the Indian market.

**INFRASTRUCTURE & CONNECTIVITY STACK**
• **Connectivity:** Deployment of ultra-low latency fiber networks and a next-gen cable landing station to handle massive cross-border AI data flows.
• **Fiber Expansion:** Significant expansion of the America–India fiber corridor to support Google’s global cloud mesh.
• **Energy:** Integration of long-term clean energy solutions, aligning with India’s 500 GW non-fossil fuel target.

**STRATEGIC VALUE CHAIN IMPACT**
• **Hyperscale Leadership:** Nxtra moves from being a colocation provider to a "Strategic Infrastructure Partner" for the world's largest tech company.
• **Revenue Mix:** Diversifies Airtel’s revenue away from pure mobility/telecom toward high-margin, long-term B2B digital infrastructure contracts.
• **AI Readiness:** Positions Nxtra at the forefront of the "Cooling Economy" and high-density power management required for AI chips.

**CORE INSIGHT**
• The Visakhapatnam project is a structural "moat-building" exercise for Airtel. By providing the "land, power, and fiber" for Google’s 1GW hub, Airtel secures a multi-decade annuity revenue stream.
• This partnership highlights the "Convergence of Giants"—where Airtel’s connectivity meets Adani’s power/land and Google’s compute demand.

**Impact: Highly Positive**

Supreme Industries: Guidance - Concall Update

For FY27, the company anticipates overall volume growth of approximately 12% to 13% and piping volume growth between 15% to 17%. || Margins for FY27 are projected to be between 14% to 14.5%.

The company proposes over INR1,000 crore in capital expenditure for the year, including carry-forward commitments, aiming to enhance manufacturing capabilities and expand capacity by approximately 110,000 metric tonnes, with 100,000 tonnes specifically for the piping sector.

New greenfield projects for plastic piping systems are planned at Patna (Bihar), Jammu (J&K), and Gadegaon (Maharashtra), along with a material handling product facility at Malanpur (Madhya Pradesh), expected to be partly operational by end of March '27 and fully in two years.

Management is confident in delivering improved performance in the coming year, citing a strong balance sheet, zero debt, expanding manufacturing base, technology leadership, and diversified business model, supported by sustained growth in the Indian economy driven by domestic consumption and infrastructure development.

reports.text:
BHARTI AIRTEL (NXTRA) – GOOGLE AI HUB PROJECT

PROJECT OVERVIEW: THE 1GW HUB
• Location: Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh.
• Scale: Groundbreaking for India’s first gigawatt-scale AI Hub.
• Investment: A cornerstone of Google’s $15 Billion "AI-First India" commitment (2026–2030).
• Significance: This is not just a data center; it is a specialized AI compute cluster designed for high-density GPU workloads.

AIRTEL’S CRITICAL ROLE (NXTRA + ADANICONNEX)
• Partnership: Nxtra by Airtel is collaborating with AdaniConneX to co-develop the campus.
• Responsibility: Airtel handles the "Data Center Lifecycle"—from construction and power management to operational connectivity.
• Infrastructure Lock-in: Solidifies Airtel’s position as the preferred infrastructure partner for global hyperscalers entering the Indian market.

INFRASTRUCTURE & CONNECTIVITY STACK
• Connectivity: Deployment of ultra-low latency fiber networks and a next-gen cable landing station to handle massive cross-border AI data flows.
• Fiber Expansion: Significant expansion of the America–India fiber corridor to support Google’s global cloud mesh.
• Energy: Integration of long-term clean energy solutions, aligning with India’s 500 GW non-fossil fuel target.

STRATEGIC VALUE CHAIN IMPACT
• Hyperscale Leadership: Nxtra moves from being a colocation provider to a "Strategic Infrastructure Partner" for the world's largest tech company.
• Revenue Mix: Diversifies Airtel’s revenue away from pure mobility/telecom toward high-margin, long-term B2B digital infrastructure contracts.
• AI Readiness: Positions Nxtra at the forefront of the "Cooling Economy" and high-density power management required for AI chips.

CORE INSIGHT
• The Visakhapatnam project is a structural "moat-building" exercise for Airtel. By providing the "land, power, and fiber" for Google’s 1GW hub, Airtel secures a multi-decade annuity revenue stream.
• This partnership highlights the "Convergence of Giants"—where Airtel’s connectivity meets Adani’s power/land and Google’s compute demand.

Impact: Highly Positive

**INVESTEC – FOOD DELIVERY & QUICK COMMERCE SECTORAL ANALYSIS**

**RATINGS & VALUATIONS**
• **Eternal (Zomato):** **Initiate BUY** | Target Price: **₹375**
• **Swiggy:** **Initiate HOLD** | Target Price: **₹310**
• **Top Pick:** Eternal is identified as the fastest-growing large-cap name in the consumer universe by both Revenue and EBITDA CAGR.

**THE EXECUTION GAP**
• **Food Delivery:** Swiggy trails Eternal by **18–24 months** in operational maturity and profitability metrics.
• **Quick Commerce:** The gap is "materially wider," with Swiggy trailing by **several years** compared to Blinkit’s execution and dark-store density.
• **Defensibility:** Food delivery is viewed as a defensible moat for now, providing the cash flow to fund the "Survival of the Fittest" war in Quick Commerce.

**EMERGING OPPORTUNITIES**
• **Asset-Light Play:** Shift toward "Emerging Platforms" (e.g., Eternal’s *District* app for going-out) is seen as a high-potential, low-capital opportunity.
• **Productivity:** Focus is shifting toward platforms that can successfully integrate non-grocery categories to boost Average Order Value (AOV).

**CORE INSIGHT**
• Investec’s preference for Eternal over Swiggy stems from the former's ability to reach a "profitability inflection point" much earlier. 
• While the market is large enough for both, Eternal’s lead in the high-stakes Quick Commerce segment (Blinkit) provides it with a structural valuation premium.

**Impact: Highly Positive (Eternal) | Neutral (Swiggy)**

RailTel Corporation of India – Bags ₹20.35 Cr Order from Directorate of Higher Education, Himachal Prades

India's Transmission Problem

Rough rule: Every 1 GW of RE needs ~0.6 GW of new inter-state transmission to actually evacuate to load centers. India needs about 280 GW of new interstate transmission capacity by 2030 to support 450 GW of solar+wind. 

Reality today:

1. 50 GW of clean energy is stranded across India that is bigger than Pakistan's entire installed grid.

2. Rajasthan alone: 8 GW stuck, with nearly half curtailed during peak solar hours.

3. FY25 transmission build: 8,830 ckm vs 15,253 ckm target, a 42% shortfall, with inter-state additions at their lowest in a decade. 

4. Solar plants commission in 12–24 months. Inter-state transmission takes 3–5 years.

5. FY26 will likely close around 10,000–12,000 ckm  missing even the revised target which was cut down by 37% in mid year, and missing the original target by 50%. Two consecutive years of severe underbuild while installed RE crossed 250 GW.

India isn't power-deficient. It's transmission deficient.

Close to 15 IT companies have had the concalls till now

Among them Persistent has been the most optimistic, HCL Tech among the most pessimistic

reports.text:
**MACQUARIE GROUP – QUICK COMMERCE SECTORAL ANALYSIS**

**INDUSTRY DYNAMICS & COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE**
• **High Intensity:** Segment-wide competition is expected to remain elevated, fueled by rapid geographic expansion and diversifying product categories.
• **Amazon’s Move:** Amazon Now is scaling to **100 cities** in India, backed by a **$300 Mn** investment commitment. This signals a shift from "optional" to "core" for the global e-commerce giant in the Indian market.
• **Price Wars:** Aggressive scaling by deep-pocketed incumbents is likely to trigger renewed customer acquisition battles and heavy discounting.

**OPERATIONAL & ECONOMIC CONCERNS**
• **Dark-Store Fragility:** Rapid expansion of dark-store networks is outpacing immediate demand growth in some micro-markets, leading to underutilization.
• **Unit Economics:** Pressure on "Take Rates" and rising delivery partner incentives are straining the path to sustainable profitability.
• **Efficiency Hurdles:** Delivery cost efficiency remains the primary bottleneck as traffic congestion and labor costs rise across Tier-1 and Tier-2 cities.

**STRATEGIC EXECUTION PRESSURES**
• **Last-Mile Logistics:** The battle is shifting from "who has the product" to "who has the fastest, most reliable delivery fleet."
• **Inventory Risk:** Expanding into higher-margin non-grocery categories (Electronics, Apparel) increases inventory holding risk and returns management complexity.

**CORE INSIGHT**
• While Quick Commerce (QC) has achieved high consumer adoption, the **"Amazon Entry"** at scale shifts the game from a growth race to a capital endurance race.
• Existing players like Zepto, Blinkit, and Swiggy Instamart face a double-edged sword: they must expand to protect territory, but every new dark store temporarily dilutes their margin profile.
• Unit economics will remain under fire until the industry moves toward a higher **Average Order Value (AOV)** by including high-value non-staple goods.

**Impact: Negative (Sectoral Margin Compression)**

THE INDIA COOLING ECONOMY – STRUCTURAL THEME TRACKER

1. CONSUMER COOLING (THE RETAIL ENGINE)
• ACs & Refrigeration: Voltas, Blue Star, Havells (Lloyd), Johnson Controls-Hitachi.
• Fans & Coolers: Symphony, Crompton Greaves, V-Guard Industries.
• The Thesis: Shifting from "discretionary" to "essential" due to extreme heatwaves and urbanization; massive runway given current 7-9% AC penetration.

2. INDUSTRIAL & DATA CENTER COOLING (THE INFRA ENGINE)
• EPC & Process Cooling: Thermax, Isgec Heavy Engineering.
• Heat Exchangers: KRN Heat Exchanger (Specialist), Patels Airtemp, Shree Refrigerations.
• HVAC & Chillers: Blue Star, Voltas, Johnson Controls-Hitachi.
• Pumps: Kirloskar Brothers.
• The AI Tailwinds: Hyperscale Data Centers require 24/7 liquid cooling; thermal management now accounts for ~40% of DC energy consumption.

3. MANUFACTURING & COMPONENT BACKBONE (THE PLI LAYER)
• Contract Manufacturers (ODMs): Amber Enterprises (Critical components), PG Electroplast, EPACK Durable.
• Electronics: Dixon Technologies.
• Value Add: Moving from "screw-driver assembly" to high-value component manufacturing (motors, PCBs, heat exchangers) under PLI schemes.

4. ANCILLARY & ELECTRIFICATION (THE MULTIPLIER)
• Cables & Wires: Polycab India, KEI Industries.
• Rationale: Every cooling installation acts as a multiplier for electrification; driving demand for specialized, high-load carrying wires and circuit protection.

CORE INSIGHT
• Cooling is the "New Electricity Proxy." It sits at the intersection of Climate Change, Urban Consumption, and AI Infrastructure.
• The theme offers both immediate seasonal demand (Retail) and long-term structural capex (Industrial/Data Centers), making it a multi-decade compounding play.

Impact: Highly Positive

reports.text:
INFOBEANS TECHNOLOGIES – AI PRODUCT LAUNCH: RAI

PRODUCT OVERVIEW: RAI (RELIABILITY & ASSURANCE INTELLIGENCE)
• Positioning: An AI-native QA agent specifically designed to validate AI-generated code within CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment) pipelines.
• Core Purpose: To bridge the "trust gap" in software development where AI-generated code often requires extensive manual review before deployment.
• Value Proposition: Reduces manual QA cycles while ensuring high reliability for agentic-generated code.

TECHNOLOGICAL ARCHITECTURE
• Layer 1: Deterministic Core Platform: Provides a stable, predictable foundation for testing workflows.
• Layer 2: Embedded QA Domain Intelligence: Incorporates deep testing logic and industry-standard QA protocols.
• Layer 3: Multi-Agent (Agentic) Workflows: Autonomous agents that can simulate complex user behavior and system stress.
• Engine: Powered by the Qi core engine, a sophisticated pytest-based CLI for seamless automation.

ECOSYSTEM & INTEGRATION
• AI Synergy: Native compatibility with Claude.ai and Claude Code (Anthropic ecosystem).
• Workflow Tools: Deep integration with Jira, Trello, and Allure for defect tracking and reporting.
• Testing Breadth: Comprehensive coverage across UI, API, Database, and Performance layers.

BUSINESS MODEL: SERVICES-AS-SOFTWARE (SaS)
• The Pivot: Moves from traditional labor-intensive billing to a hybrid model combining high-margin software tools with managed QA expertise.
• Moat: Proprietary technology (RAI) paired with 2,000+ specialized professionals creates a high entry barrier for pure-play software competitors.

STRATEGIC RECOGNITION & STRENGTH
• Award: Named ServiceNow APAC Transformation Partner of the Year 2026, validating their high-tier enterprise execution.
• Global Reach: Operations across India, US, and Europe provide a diverse 24/7 delivery model.

CORE INSIGHT
• InfoBeans is aggressively carving out a niche in the AI Validation layer**—a segment that is becoming mandatory as enterprises scale their use of LLMs for coding.
• By aligning with the Claude ecosystem, they are positioning themselves as a specialized alternative to the broader Microsoft/GitHub Copilot testing stack.
• The "Services-as-Software" model is a smart structural play to improve revenue-per-employee and drive valuation re-rating beyond traditional IT services multiples.

**Impact: Highly Positive

KNOWLEDGE MARINE & ENGINEERING WORKS (KMEW) – GROWTH TRACKER

SHIPYARD & SHIPBUILDING PIVOT
• Investment: Investing ~₹100 Cr into a dedicated shipyard to build tugs and small-to-mid vessels.
• Revenue Target: Management targeting ₹500–700 Cr (3x FY25 revenue) from this segment within 3 years.
• Execution: Already reported ₹26.5 Cr revenue from newly operational shipbuilding activities in Q3 FY26 alone.
• Strategic Shift: Moving from "hiring" vessels to "building and owning," which is expected to structurally enhance long-term margins.

DREDGING MARKET OPPORTUNITY (THE TAM)
• Total Addressable Market (TAM): Exceeds ₹3,000 Cr annually across major and minor Indian ports.
• River Dredging Boom: As 20+ National Waterways become operational (Budget 2026 push), the river dredging market is projected to scale from ₹1,500 Cr to ₹5,000 Cr.
• Headroom: KMEW’s current dredging revenue is <₹200 Cr, leaving massive runway for market share capture.
• Competitive Edge: High entry barriers due to stringent pre-qualification norms (track record and technical seabed data).

FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE & OUTLOOK (FY26)
• FY26 Estimates: Revenue projected at ~₹260 Cr (↑ 30% YoY).
• Order Book: Robust pipeline of ₹1,500 Cr (as of March 2026) with an additional ₹3,000 Cr in active bids.
• Profitability: Forecasted PAT CAGR of 71% (FY25–28E) as high-margin dredging and shipbuilding contracts scale.
• Tax Efficiency: Successfully transitioned to the "Tonnage Tax Scheme" (AY 2026-27), which significantly lowers the effective tax rate and boosts free cash flow.

CAPITAL ALLOCATION & GOVERNANCE
• Borrowing Limit: Recently increased from ₹500 Cr to ₹1,200 Cr (March 2026) to fund the shipyard and fleet expansion.
• Cash Surplus: Deployed ~₹273 Cr from preferential issues into FDs as a "war chest" for vessel acquisition.
• Institutional Interest: Institutional stake increased by ~2% recently, reflecting confidence in the transition.

CORE INSIGHT
• KMEW is no longer just a "small-cap dredging firm"; it is becoming a "Marine Infrastructure Proxy."
• The expansion into the shipyard business creates a self-sustaining cycle—building its own fleet at cost while selling specialized vessels (like green tugs) to ports.
• The ₹3,000 Cr+ dredging opportunity in inland waterways is the most significant "alpha" generator over the next 5 years.

Impact: Highly Positive

NIPPON LIFE INDIA AMC (NAM-INDIA) – CONTRA CASE STUDY

HISTORICAL ENTRY RATIONALE (THE CONTRA VIEW)
• Valuation Moat: Entry at 16 PE during peak pessimism (fears of Zerodha/Jio entry and TER cuts).
• The "Two-Wheeler" Analogy: Identifying the sector in its 1990s-style infancy, with a multi-decade compounding runway.
• Sentiment Filter: Low webinar attendance (61 people) acted as a bullish signal of extreme under-ownership.

THE 5-POINT FUNDAMENTAL THESIS
1. Structural Tailwinds: Financialization of Indian savings ensuring 15%+ AUM growth.
2. Product Alpha: Leadership in passives and ETFs (now ~33% of their AUM).
3. Sticky Inflows: High-conviction SIP book (~₹447 Bn annualized) and "no-nonsense" fund management.
4. Asset Balance: Prudent mix between Equity and Debt, avoiding the "yield-chasing" trap.
5. Governance: Refusal to engage in toxic NFOs or low-quality AUM gathering during bull market peaks.

BUSINESS MODEL SUPERIORITY
• Uniformity: Consistent revenue streams compared to the "feast or famine" cycles of Investment Banking.
• Sustainability: Higher terminal value due to the "sticky" nature of AUM compared to the price-sensitive nature of discount brokerage.
• Profitability: Asset-light model with best-in-class dividend payouts (~91% in FY26).

OPERATIONAL MILESTONES (AS OF Q4 FY26)
• AUM Scale: Total closing AUM at ₹7.73 Trillion.
• Market Share: 8.89% (Rising for several consecutive quarters).
• Efficiency: ~58 new SIPs per minute, highlighting massive retail penetration.

CORE INSIGHT
• NAM-INDIA has transitioned from a "mispriced contra bet" to a "core compounding machine." 
• The deep research—studying 40 years of global MF history—provided the "informational edge" needed to hold through the noise of fee-compression narratives.
• At a potential 5x return, it validates the strategy that one well-researched contra bet can fund the "latency" of 3–4 other stagnant portfolio positions.

Impact: Highly Positive (Conviction Re-rating)

reports.text:
RBI FINALSES ECL NORMS: APRIL 2027 ROLLOUT FOR PROACTIVE PROVISIONING

• RBI mandates Expected Credit Loss (ECL) framework effective April 1, 2027
• Replaces reactive "incurred-loss" model with "forward-looking" credit risk recognition
• Transition impact adjusted against opening retained earnings, not through P&L
• Three-stage classification: Stage 1 (Standard), Stage 2 (High-Risk), Stage 3 (Impaired)
• Minimum provisions: 0.4% for Stage 1 and 5% for Stage 2
• ECL calculated via Probability of Default, Loss Given Default, and Exposure
• Banks must use probability-weighted estimates across multiple macroeconomic scenarios
• Transition impact spread over four years, ending March 31, 2031
• 90-day delinquency norm retained for consistent NPA identification across sectors
• Mandatory fair valuation of entire loan portfolios on the transition date

Impact: Negative to Neutral – Short-term capital hit likely; long-term structural banking stability improves

NVIDIA VS. HYPERSCALERS: THE SHIFT TO CUSTOM AI SILICON

• Nvidia's monopoly fractures as hyperscalers build in-house AI chips
• Google TPU forecast: 2.7M units vs. Nvidia's 9.4M GPUs
• Amazon-Anthropic alliance: $25B investment in 5 GW compute capacity
• Meta's MTIA chips expected to cut ownership costs by 44%
• Tesla-Samsung $16.5B deal for AI5/AI6 chips by late 2026
• Huawei Ascend 910C and CANN architecture rival Nvidia's CUDA
• OpenAI-Broadcom partnership targets custom silicon production by 2026
• Shift from general GPUs to model-specific, cost-efficient ASICs
• Major labs move toward silicon independence and compute sovereignty

ZENSAR TECHNOLOGIES Q4 FY26 & OUTLOOK SUMMARY

FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE (Q4 FY26)
• Net Profit (PAT): ₹210.6 Cr (↑ 19.4% YoY | ↑ 5.6% QoQ) — Beat Estimates
• Revenue (USD): $158.4 Mn (↑ 1.0% YoY | ↓ 1.3% QoQ)
• EBITDA Margin: 14.7% (↓ 130 bps QoQ) — *Impacted by lower working days and large deal transition costs.*
• Dividend: Final dividend of ₹12.60 per share (630% on ₹2 FV).

ORDER BOOK & DEAL PIPELINE
• Record Order Book: $401.8 Mn (↑ 122.9% QoQ) — All-time high driven by a significant strategic large deal win.
• AI-Led Pipeline: Management noted that a substantial portion of new order bookings is now "AI-influenced."
• Vertical Trends: Banking, Financial Services, and Insurance (BFSI) remain strong; Telecom, Media, and Technology (TMT) continue to face revenue headwinds (↓ 9.7% for the full year).

AI IMPACT & WORKFORCE STRATEGY
• Hiring Outlook: Management expects muted hiring in the near term as AI-led productivity begins to ramp up.
• Fresher Impact: AI tools are compressing effort for entry-level tasks, leading to a potential structural shift in fresher intake.
• Reskilling: 85% of the workforce is now AI-certified, as the company transitions to an "AI-embedded" delivery model.

STRATEGIC VIEW ON SAAS & APPS
• SaaS Resilience: Contrary to market fears of a "wipeout," Zensar believes SaaS companies will coexist, though their delivery models will evolve.
• Product Shift: Expects a trend where rigid SaaS products are increasingly replaced by agile, AI-integrated custom applications tailored to specific enterprise outcomes.

GUIDANCE & OUTLOOK
• Q1 FY27: Management expects Q1 FY27 to be better than Q4 FY26, signaling the start of a recovery cycle.
• Margins: Aiming to sustain operating margins in the mid-teens range for FY27, despite transition costs from new large deals.
• Cash Position: Strong net cash position of $319.5 Mn, providing a buffer for potential M&A and continued high dividend payouts.

CORE INSIGHT
• Zensar is successfully pivoting into a "mid-tier large deal" player, evidenced by its record $401M order book. 
• While near-term revenue growth is modest (3.1% for FY26), the transition to an AI-native delivery model is helping protect margins and drive higher per-employee productivity.
• The aggressive dividend payout (91.5% for the year) underscores the RPG Group's confidence in the company's "cash cow" status.

Impact: Positive

ORDER BOOK & REVENUE VISIBILITY • Consolidated Order Book: ₹900+ Cr (as of March 31, 2026)  
• Hot Lead Pipeline: ~$455 Million (High conversion potential)  
• Ticket Size: Significant shift to larger projects ($7–10 Mn vs. previous $1.5–5 Mn)  
• FY26 Revenue Guidance: ₹380–400 Cr (Targeting 30% YoY growth for FY27)  

INTERNATIONAL PROJECT WINS • West Africa – OSD Facility: • Value: ~$63.6 Cr (USD 7.05 Mn)  
  • Scope: Supply, installation, and commissioning of Oral Solid Dosage (OSD) infrastructure  
  • Support: Backed by IFC and UNIDO to reduce local medicine dependency  
• Saudi Arabia – Animal Vaccine Project: • Value: ~$65.5 Cr (USD 7.8 Mn)  
  • Scope: Turnkey cleanroom, HVAC, MEP, and utilities through handover  
  • Timeline: ~11 months (Entry into the high-growth animal health segment)  
• North Africa – Veterinary Facility: • Value: ₹49–52 Cr  
  • Scope: EU-GMP compliant veterinary formulations facility  
  • Timeline: Target completion by Q1 FY27 (Strategic for EU export access)  

STRATEGIC DIVERSIFICATION & MOAT • Segment Pivot: Expanding beyond core Pharma into Animal Health, Renewable Energy (₹330 Cr solar contract), and Data Centers.  
• Integration: Recent acquisition of Kelvin Air Conditioning (51%) enhances in-house HVAC and MEP capabilities.  
• Global Reach: Presence in 62 countries with a focus on "self-reliance" projects in the MEA region post-COVID.  

[Image of pharmaceutical cleanroom HVAC and MEP systems]

FINANCIAL HEALTH • Cash Position: Net cash positive transition following the January 2025 IPO.  
• Operating Leverage: Transition to larger ticket sizes ($10 Mn+) is expected to drive margin expansion as field force productivity improves.  

CORE INSIGHT • Fabtech has successfully graduated from small equipment supply to massive turnkey engineering projects, evidenced by the ₹900 Cr+ order book.  
• The strategic entry into the Animal Health space (KSA and North Africa) provides a high-margin, less-crowded vertical compared to generic human pharma.  
• Strong backing from global bodies like the IFC for African projects significantly reduces payment/sovereign risk for their international receivables.  

Impact: Highly Positive

**ULTRATECH CEMENT – GOLDMAN SACHS ANALYSIS**

**RATING & VALUATION** • **Rating:** Maintain **BUY** • **Target Price:** ₹13,230 (Revised from ₹13,640)  
• **EBITDA CAGR:** ~14% expected over the next 2 years (FY26–FY28).  

**Q4 FY26 PERFORMANCE METRICS** • **EBITDA per Tonne:** ₹1,167 (Strong performance beating estimates).  
• **Volume Growth:** 9% YoY (Total) | India Cement volumes up 18% YoY.  
• **Realizations:** Improved ~3% QoQ, showcasing pricing power in a competitive market.  
• **Cost Efficiency:** Sequential EBITDA/t expansion driven by strong cost control measures.  

**STRATEGIC CONSOLIDATION** • **Brand Integration:** Successful integration of **India Cements** and **Kesoram** assets completed.  
• **Capacity:** Aggressive roadmap to 200+ MTPA solidifies market leadership.  
• **Efficiency:** Focus on green power (43% mix) and alternative fuels to offset inflationary pressures.  

**RISKS & OUTLOOK** • **Energy Headwinds:** Near-term margins face pressure from higher energy and fuel costs (West Asia conflict impact).  
• **Growth Pipeline:** Long-term outlook remains robust, supported by aggressive capacity additions and operational efficiencies.  

**CORE INSIGHT** • Goldman Sachs maintains a bullish stance on UltraTech, viewing it as the primary beneficiary of India's multi-year infrastructure cycle.  
• While the target price saw a minor downward revision due to energy cost sensitivities, the operational "beat" on EBITDA per tonne (₹1,167) reinforces the company’s ability to manage costs better than peers.  
• The completion of key acquisitions (India Cements/Kesoram) positions them to capture a larger share of the southern and western markets.

**Impact: Positive**

AU SMALL FINANCE BANK – Q4 FY26 CONCALL UPDATE

PROFITABILITY & ROA GUIDANCE • Target: Management aims to sustain the 1.8% ROA (Q4 exit rate) on a full-year basis for FY27.  
• Drivers: Growth in profitability will be fueled by improving operating leverage (OpEx/Assets) and stabilizing credit costs.  

CREDIT COSTS & ASSET QUALITY • FY27 Estimate: Analysts are advised to model ~90 bps for the full year.  
• Caution: Management flagged that the Q4 exit rate of 60 bps was seasonally strong and should not be used as a sustainable baseline.  
• Slippages: Q4 benefited from lower slippages and favorable day counts, which may normalize in the coming quarters.  

OPERATIONAL EFFICIENCY (OPEX) • Near-Term Target: Cost-to-Assets ratio expected to dip below 4% in the current financial year.  
• Long-Term Target: Aims for 3.5% within 3–5 years.  
• Tech Catalyst: Significant reliance on AI and automation to drive these efficiency gains.  

MARGINS & YIELDS • Cost of Funds (CoF): Management believes CoF has likely bottomed out, suggesting limited room for further interest expense reduction.  
• Yield Pressure: Potential pressure on yields due to changes in the asset mix and the absence of Q4's seasonal tailwinds.  

GROWTH & SUSTAINABILITY • Compounding: Management expects the franchise to grow at 2 to 2.5 times India's nominal GDP growth rate sustainably.  
• Universal Bank: The strategic focus remains on strengthening the foundation for a potential transition to a Universal Bank license.  

CORE INSIGHT • AU SFB is pivoting from "growth at all costs" to "profitable efficiency." The 1.8% ROA target is ambitious but backed by a clear roadmap to cut the cost-to-assets ratio via AI.  
• By guiding for 90 bps credit costs (vs the current 60 bps), management is setting a conservative floor, which provides a buffer for the stock's valuation if macro conditions fluctuate.  

Impact: Positive

RALLIS INDIA Q4 FY26 CROP CARE RESULTS

FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE (Q4 FY26) • EBITDA: ↑ 231% YoY (Massive operating leverage)  
• Net Profit (PAT): ₹3 Cr (Turnaround vs ₹11 Cr Loss YoY)  
• Overall Revenue: Driven by strong domestic volumes  

CROP CARE SEGMENT BREAKDOWN • Crop Care (Total): ↑ 5% YoY  
• B2C (Retail CP): ↑ 15% YoY — *Reflects strong brand pull and retail demand.* • Soil & Plant Health (SPH): ↑ 27% YoY — Star performer of the quarter. • B2B (Institutional): ↓ 7% YoY — *Dragging due to continued weakness in export markets.* OPERATIONAL HIGHLIGHTS • Turnaround: Shift from loss to profit driven by improved product mix and cost discipline.  
• Domestic Traction: Strong retail momentum (B2C) successfully offsetting the global B2B/export slowdown.  
• SPH Momentum: High-growth Soil & Plant Health segment becoming a significant contributor to the margin profile. 

STRATEGIC OUTLOOK • Focus: Increasing penetration in the high-margin B2C segment to reduce dependency on volatile B2B cycles.  
• Recovery: Operational turnaround signals that the worst of the inventory destocking and pricing pressure may be behind the company.  

CORE INSIGHT • Rallis has delivered a "quality" turnaround, where the bottom line has returned to the green despite a lukewarm top-line growth (5%).  
• The 27% surge in the SPH segment suggests that the company’s diversification into bio-stimulants and plant nutrition is paying off.  
• While B2B/Exports remain a laggard, the strong EBITDA rebound (231%) indicates a significantly leaner and more efficient cost structure.  

Impact: Positive

GOVT MAY TWEAK DLI SCHEME FOR SEMICONDUCTOR CHIPS AND ALLOCATE FUNDS ON A LIKE-FOR-LIKE BASIS ONLY TO CO'S OR STARTUPS THAT SUCCESSFULLY RAISE FUNDS FROM ANGEL INVESTORS OR VENTURE CAPITAL (VC) FUNDS. ( MOSCHIP TECH )

RBI ECL FRAMEWORK – FINAL DIRECTIONS & CITI ANALYSIS

IMPLEMENTATION TIMELINE
• Effective Date: April 1, 2027 (SCBs excluding SFBs and Payment Banks).
• Glide Path: A 5-year transition period (up to March 31, 2031) to absorb capital hits.
• Fair Valuation: Mandatory fair value assessment of the entire loan book on the transition date.

KEY PRUDENTIAL PROVISIONING FLOORS
• Stage 1 (Standard): - General: Aligned at 40 bps.
  - Relief: Lowered to 25 bps for Individual Home Loans and SME/Farm-related loans (CGTSME/NCGTC).
  - Risk Weight: Retained at 1% for Unsecured Retail (credit cards/personal loans).
• Stage 2 (Significant Increase in Risk): - Floor: Held at 5% for most products (despite industry lobbying).
  - Exceptions: 1.5% for Home Loans/Residential LAP/Gold Loans; 2.5% for Commercial LAP.
• Stage 3 (Credit Impaired/NPA): - Secured (Home/Gold): 10% (≤1 yr) scaling up to 100% (>4 yrs).
  - Unsecured Retail: 25% (≤1 yr) and 100% (>1 yr).

CRITICAL REGULATORY CHANGES
• Seasoning Requirement: The previously proposed 6-month "cool-off" period to move from Stage 3 back to Stage 1 has been withdrawn. This provides relief for faster asset classification recovery.
• EIR Method: Banks must adopt the Effective Interest Rate (EIR) method for income recognition by March 31, 2030.

CITI IMPACT ASSESSMENT
• Net Positive: Banks with high concentration in Home Loans (HL) benefit from the lower 25 bps Stage-1 floor and the 1.5% Stage-2 floor.
• Adverse: Banks with high 30–90 DPD (Days Past Due) exposure in Unsecured Retail, MFI, and Vehicle Finance will face higher provisioning due to the 5% Stage-2 floor.
• PSB Pressure: Public Sector Banks (PSBs) may face higher capital pressure due to older vintage Stage-3 accounts and historical staging data gaps.

CORE INSIGHT
• The ECL framework forces banks to recognize stress earlier. The retention of a 5% floor for Stage-2 is a conservative stance by the RBI to prevent "under-provisioning" in volatile retail segments.
• Withdrawal of the seasoning requirement is a major operational win for banks, allowing them to reclassify cured accounts immediately.

Impact: Positive for Large Private Banks (HL focus) | Neutral-Negative for MFIs/PSBs

IZMO LTD (IZMO MICROSYSTEMS) – SILICON PHOTONICS MISSION

THE ANNOUNCEMENT (APRIL 27, 2026)
• Official Partner: izmo Microsystems (subsidiary of Izmo Ltd) recognized as the "Photonic IC Packaging Partner" for the National Silicon Photonics Mission.
• Collaboration: Led by the Center of Excellence for Compound Photonics and Photonic Integrated Circuits Systems (CoE-CPPICS) at IIT Madras.
• Governance: Formally disclosed via SEBI Regulation 30 filings to BSE and NSE.

THE TECHNOLOGICAL STACK (WHAT'S READY)
• Design PDK: Indigenously developed Process Design Kit (PDK) with over 50 verified components (Launch: April 24, 2026).
• Test Engine: Universal Programmable Photonic IC (PPIC) automated test platform ready for module characterization.
• Foundry Partner: SilTerra Malaysia (CMOS-compatible silicon photonics foundry).
• Packaging (The Izmo Link): Specialized sub-micron precision packaging required to convert raw photonic chips into functional, deployable systems.

STRATEGIC ROLE OF IZMO MICROSYSTEMS
• Function: Handles heterogeneous integration (combining light-based and electronic components) and 3D System-in-Package (SiP).
• Capability: Precision active alignment of fiber arrays, waveguides, and laser sources—a critical bottleneck in commercializing Silicon Photonics.
• Upcoming Milestones: Multi-Project Wafer (MPW) fabrication runs starting Q3 FY26, including comprehensive testing and module characterization.

MARKET APPLICATIONS & TAM
• AI & HPC: Hardware acceleration for deep learning via co-packaged optics.
• Data Centers: Terabit-scale optical communication to reduce latency and power.
• Quantum Computing: Secure photonic networking and quantum-regime sensing.
• Telecom: Next-gen 5G/6G low-loss, high-density interconnects.

CORE INSIGHT
• While the market typically chases "Fabs," Izmo has captured the "Packaging" layer—a high-margin, low-competition segment essential for technology sovereignty.
• Without Izmo’s sub-micron packaging, the IIT Madras designs remain theoretical. This makes Izmo a "toll-gate" partner in India's deep-tech semiconductor stack.
• This transition from a "marketing solutions" company to a "semiconductor packaging" leader represents a structural shift in Izmo's business profile.

Impact: Highly Positive

Nobody knows anything!

It is often repeated like gospel truth that when promoters are buying, you should buy. When insiders are buying, you should buy. When companies issue bonus shares, it signals confidence from the management team.

But reality is far more complicated.

Just look at the buybacks by three leading IT firms over the last few years. Together, they bought back shares worth nearly ₹1,11,500 crores at various price levels.

At current prices, with hindsight, these companies could have saved almost ₹32,000 crores.

So were those buybacks a signal of future returns? Clearly, not necessarily.

This shows that interpreting promoter buying, insider activity, bonus issues, or buybacks as guaranteed indicators of future performance is often just hindsight dressed as wisdom.

What really matters in stocks are the following:

Sales growth
Conversion of sales into profits
Conversion of profits into free cash flow
Protection of minority shareholders’ rights

Among all of these, the most critical is revenue growth.

Because without growth in the underlying business, everything else is just an exercise in filling blank spaces in annual reports.

The rest may help, but growth is the foundation.

WEBSOL ENERGY SYSTEM Q4 FY26 RESULTS

FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE (Q4 FY26) • Revenue: ₹401 Cr (↑ 132.1% YoY | ↑ 53.8% QoQ)  
• EBITDA: ₹146 Cr (↑ 86.4% YoY | ↑ 37.4% QoQ)  
• EBITDA Margin: 36.4% (vs 45.4% YoY | 40.8% QoQ)  
• PBT: ₹125 Cr (↑ 89.2% YoY)  
• PAT: ₹125 Cr (↑ 157.9% YoY | ↑ 91.6% QoQ)  
• EPS: ₹2.80 (↑ 140.4% YoY)

FULL YEAR PERFORMANCE (FY26) • Revenue: ₹1,049 Cr (↑ 82.4% YoY)  
• EBITDA: ₹429 Cr (↑ 69.6% YoY)  
• EBITDA Margin: 40.8% (vs 43.9% YoY)  
• PAT: ₹303 Cr (↑ 95.8% YoY)  
• EPS: ₹7.00 (↑ 90.4% YoY)

CAPACITY & OPERATIONAL UPDATES • Technology Upgrade: Converting Mono PERC line to Topcon technology; Overall capacity to reach 1.35 GW.  
• Utilization: Cell Line-2 ramped successfully; Cell utilization at >90%, Module utilization at ~74%.  
• Order Book: Strong visibility with ₹1,161 Cr in unexecuted orders.  
• Balance Sheet: Turned Net Cash Positive with ₹34 Cr net cash (₹152 Cr cash vs ₹118 Cr debt).  

STRATEGIC ROADMAP & OUTLOOK • Future Goal: Build towards a 2 GW integrated cell + module facility.  
• Backward Integration: Management focusing on internalizing more of the value chain to protect margins.  
• Sector Tailwinds: Benefiting from ALMM policies and PLI schemes driving demand for high-efficiency domestic solar products.  

CORE INSIGHT • Websol has successfully transitioned from a turnaround story to a high-execution growth phase, evidenced by record revenue and turning net cash positive.  
• While margins saw a slight YoY contraction (likely due to tech-upgrade transition costs), the 157% PAT growth signals massive operating leverage.  
• The shift to Topcon technology is a crucial competitive move to maintain relevance as the industry moves away from older Mono PERC standards.  

Impact: Highly Positive

NDTV PROFIT EARNINGS WATCHLIST: Q4 FY26

POSITIVE MOMENTUM

• COAL INDIA: PAT ↑ 11% to ₹10,839 Cr. Revenue ↑ 6% to ₹46,490 Cr. Driven by higher FSA realizations and e-auction volumes. Final dividend of ₹5.25/share declared.
• ADANI TOTAL GAS: Revenue ↑ 16% to ₹1,696 Cr. Volumes ↑ 13% YoY despite West Asia geopolitical disruptions. Calibration in passing cost hikes supported volume growth.
• PIRAMAL FINANCE: PAT ↑ 9x to ₹603 Cr (aided by exceptional gains). Total Income ↑ 56.5% to ₹4,783 Cr. Significant turnaround with strong recovery momentum.
• AU SMALL FINANCE: PAT ↑ 65% to ₹832 Cr. GNPA improved to 2.03%. ROA expanded to 1.8%. Strategic shift to secured assets driving profitability.
• CITY UNION BANK: PAT ↑ 25% to ₹360 Cr. GNPA improved to 1.91%. Announced a 1:3 bonus issue and ₹2 dividend/share. Margins at multi-quarter highs.
• PHOENIX MILLS: Strong retail consumption growth continues to drive rental income and occupancy across premium mall assets.
• WEBSOL ENERGY: PAT surges 3x YoY. Revenue consensus at ₹420–480 Cr. Benefiting from massive demand in the solar cell/wafer sector and PLI momentum.
• PRATAAP SNACKS: Turned profitable with PAT of ₹1.1 Cr (vs ₹11.9 Cr loss). EBITDA margin expanded to 4.9% vs 1.2% YoY.
• ASTEC LIFESCIENCES: Net loss narrowed to ₹7.8 Cr (vs ₹16.1 Cr loss YoY). Revenue ↑ 32.7% to ₹159 Cr. EBITDA margins improved to 5.76%.
• ROSSARI BIOTECH: Net Profit ↑ 33% YoY. Strong volume growth in Home, Personal Care, and Performance Chemicals (HPPC) segment.

NEGATIVE MOMENTUM

• JINDAL SAW: PAT ↓ 52% to ₹139.4 Cr. Revenue ↓ 8% to ₹4,634 Cr. EBITDA margins compressed sharply to 10.4% (vs 14.6% YoY) due to higher input costs and lower realizations.

CORE INSIGHT
• Financials (AU SFB, TMB, CUB) and Asset Managers (Nippon) show dominant growth, while manufacturing turnarounds (Prataap, Websol) are gaining traction. 
• Industrial/Pipes (Jindal Saw) face near-term margin pressure from global macro headwinds.

Impact: Mixed to Positive

AU SMALL FINANCE BANK Q4 FY26 RESULTS

BUSINESS SCALE & DEPOSITS • Deposits: ₹1,52,661 Cr (↑ 23% YoY | ↑ 10% QoQ)  
• CASA Deposits: ₹43,357 Cr (↑ 20% YoY | ↑ 9% QoQ)  
• Advances: ₹1,40,327 Cr (↑ 21% YoY | ↑ 8% QoQ)  
• Portfolio Mix: Secured book grew 23% YoY; Unsecured book declined 1% YoY (Strategic de-risking)

INCOME & PROFITABILITY • Net Interest Income (NII): ₹2,582 Cr (↑ 23% YoY)  
• PPOP: ₹1,352 Cr (↑ 5% YoY | ↑ 11% QoQ)  
• PAT: ₹832 Cr (↑ 65% YoY | ↑ 25% QoQ) — Sharp outperformance • Provisions: ₹269 Cr (↓ 58% YoY) — *Significant reduction in credit costs aiding the bottom line.*

MARGINS & RETURNS • NIM: 6.0% (vs 5.8% YoY | 5.7% QoQ)  
• ROA: 1.8% (vs 1.4% YoY | 1.6% QoQ)  
• ROE: 17.0% (vs 11.9% YoY | 14.3% QoQ)  

ASSET QUALITY • GNPA: 2.03% (improving from 2.28% YoY | 2.30% QoQ)  
• NNPA: 0.74% (stable YoY | improving from 0.88% QoQ)  
• Slippages: ₹659 Cr (↓ 17% QoQ)  
• Collection Efficiency: 99.7% in non-overdue MFI loans (Improving trend)

TECHNOLOGY & DISTRIBUTION • Digital Push: Launched Agentic AI platform and AI-native loan origination system.  
• Footprint: Added 334 touchpoints in FY26, including 78 liability branches in urban markets.  
• Transition: Management confident about the roadmap toward a Universal Bank license.

MANAGEMENT VIEW • Focused on building a "Forever Bank" with disciplined growth.  
• Decadal milestone achieved with a shift toward tech-led banking transformation.  
• Strategic pivot towards secured assets is paying off in asset quality metrics.

CORE INSIGHT • AU SFB has delivered an exceptionally strong Q4, characterized by sharp expansion in ROA (1.8%) and ROE (17%).  
• The successful integration of technology (Agentic AI) and the cooling of slippages suggest a highly sustainable growth trajectory.  
• Improving NIMs (6.0%) despite a competitive deposit environment highlights the bank's strong liability franchise.

Impact: Positive

TAMILNAD MERCANTILE BANK Q4 FY26 RESULTS

BUSINESS SCALE & GROWTH • Total Business: ₹1,15,091 Cr (↑ 17.37% YoY)  
• Deposits: ₹61,712 Cr (↑ 14.94% YoY | ↑ 8.83% QoQ)  
• Advances: ₹53,379 Cr (↑ 20.32% YoY)  
• CASA: ₹17,365 Cr (↑ 22.35% YoY) — *Reflects strong low-cost deposit mobilization.* LOAN BOOK COMPOSITION (RAM FOCUS) • RAM Advances: ₹50,528 Cr (95% of total book)  
• Retail: ↑ 62% YoY (Aggressive expansion in retail segment)  
• MSME: ↑ 15% YoY  
• Agriculture: ↑ 8% YoY  

INCOME & PROFITABILITY • Net Interest Income (NII): ₹704.45 Cr (↑ 24.04% YoY)

• Other Income: ₹1,792.16 Cr (Total Income ↑ 16.22% YoY)  

MARGINS & RETURNS • NIM: 4.18% (vs 3.91% YoY | 4.04% QoQ)  
• ROA: 2.05% (vs 1.81% YoY | 1.97% QoQ)  
• ROE: 15.03% (vs 13.79% YoY | 14.22% QoQ)  

ASSET QUALITY • GNPA: 0.73% (Significant improvement from 1.25% YoY)  
• NNPA: 0.18% (Best-in-class | vs 0.36% YoY)  
• Slippages: Consistent improving trend across all quarters.  

VALUATION & TECHNICALS • Book Value: ₹638.40 per share  
• Price to Book (P/B): 1.07x (Trading at attractive valuations relative to performance)  
• Technical Status: Stock trading at All-Time High (ATH) with strong price momentum.  

CORE INSIGHT • Tamilnad Mercantile Bank (TMB) has delivered a stellar "all-round" performance with accelerated growth in high-yield retail advances and record-low NPAs.  
• The sharp expansion in NIM (4.18%) and ROA (2.05%) positions it as one of the most efficient private banks in the mid-cap space.  
• Strong CASA growth and a high-RAM focus provide a structural advantage for sustained margin protection.  

Impact: Positive

NIPPON LIFE INDIA AMC Q4 FY26 RESULTS

ASSETS UNDER MANAGEMENT (AUM) • Total Closing AUM: ₹7.73 Trillion  
• MF QAAUM: ₹7.25 Lakh Cr (↑ 30% YoY | ↑ 3% QoQ)  
• Market Share: 8.89% (↑ 63 bps YoY | ↑ 24 bps QoQ)  
• ETF AUM: ₹2.42 Lakh Cr (↑ 57% YoY | Market Share: 21.4%)  

SIP & RETAIL MOMENTUM • Systematic Flows: ₹108.7 Billion (↑ 12% YoY)  
• Annualized Systematic Book: ~₹447 Billion  
• Unique Investors: 23.8 Million (MF industry leader in reach)  
• Transaction Velocity: ~58 new SIPs per minute  

FINANCIAL PERFORMANCE (Q4 FY26) • Revenue: ₹738.70 Cr (↑ 30% YoY | ↑ 5% QoQ)  
• Core Operating Profit: ₹493.30 Cr (↑ 39% YoY)  
• PAT: ₹384.70 Cr (↑ 29% YoY | ↓ 5% QoQ)  
• Other Income: -₹33.5 Cr (Impacted by MTM/Treasury volatility)  

SHAREHOLDER REWARDS • Final Dividend: ₹12.50 per share (Subject to approval)  
• Total FY26 Dividend: ₹21.50 per share  
• Dividend Payout: 91.5% of standalone earnings (Highly capital-efficient)  
• Record Date: June 26, 2026 | Payment Date: From July 10, 2026  

DIGITAL & OPERATIONAL REACH • Digital Contribution: 77% of total purchases and SIPs  
• Transactions: 5.04 Million digital transactions in Q4 alone  
• Folio Base: 39.4 Million (Added 2.7 Million folios in just one quarter)  

CORE INSIGHT • Nippon Life AMC has delivered a robust core operating performance with best-in-class market share gains in both Equity and ETF segments.  
• Despite a dip in treasury-led 'Other Income', the core business is firing on all cylinders, driven by aggressive retail expansion and a massive SIP book.  
• The high dividend payout ratio reinforces management's commitment to returning capital in an asset-light business model.  

Impact: Positive

INCOME & PROFITABILITY • Net Interest Income (NII): ₹945 Cr (↑ 15% YoY)

• NNPA: 0.11% (stable YoY and QoQ)  
• Asset Quality: Remains industry-leading with negligible slippages.  

STRATEGIC MOVES & MARKET POSITION • Segment Focus: Massive traction in Lease Rental Discounting (LRD) indicates a shift toward high-yield commercial assets.  
• Market Share: Now established as the 2nd largest Housing Finance Company (HFC) in India by AUM.  
• Efficiency: Strong Pre-provision Operating Profit growth (23%) demonstrates high operational leverage.  

CORE INSIGHT • Bajaj Housing Finance continues its aggressive AUM growth while maintaining "gold standard" asset quality (0.27% GNPA).  
• The sharp 44% growth in LRD is successfully diversifying the book beyond vanilla home loans, aiding margin stability in a competitive environment.  

Impact: Positive

Hindustan Zinc — Systematix Snapshot

• Rating: **BUY**  
• TP: **Rs 724**; CMP: **Rs 589**; **22%** upside  

• Valuation: SOTP; zinc/lead 9x EV/EBITDA, silver 12x  
• 4QFY26 Revenue: **Rs 126.9bn** (+43.8% YoY)  

• 4QFY26 EBITDA: **Rs 77.1bn**; margin **56.9%**  

• 4QFY26 PAT: **Rs 50bn** (+68% YoY)  

• FY26 Revenue/EBITDA/PAT: **408/220.6/138.3 bn**  
• Zinc CoP ex‑royalty: **USD 903/t** (4Q), FY26 **USD 959/t**  

• Silver price avg: **USD 84/oz**; silver rev **Rs 40.3bn** (4Q)  
• Volumes: mined **315kt** (4Q); zinc metal **227kt** (4Q)  

• Guidance FY27: mined **1,150kt**, refined **1,100kt**, Ag **680t**  

• FY27 capex: **USD 0.5–0.6bn**; FY26 capex **Rs 36bn**  
• Dividend yield guided ~**5.1%** (FY27–28)  

• Key catalysts: silver tailwinds, CoP savings, smelter ramps  

• Key risks: silver price reversal; project execution delays; geopolitics

CIE Automotive India — Mirae Asset / Sharekhan Highlights

• Rating: Positive; Target **Rs 552** (~**21%** upside)  
• CMP referenced: **Rs 457**  

• Valuation: **16.3x/14.8x/13.6x** CY26/27/28 EPS  
• Market cap: **~Rs 17,300 crore**; 52W **526 / 381**  

• Q1CY26 revenue: **Rs 2,612 cr** (+14.9% YoY)  
• Q1CY26 EBITDA: **Rs 401.9 cr**; margin **15.4%**  
• Q1CY26 PAT: **Rs 248.1 cr**; EPS **6.5**  

• India revenue: **Rs 1,663.5 cr**; Europe: **Rs 948.5 cr**  
• Europe aided by ~**17%** forex benefit  

• CY26 capex plan: **Rs 400–500 cr** (Rs 100 cr spent)  

• Management aims to outperform industry CY26  
• Europe restructuring largely complete; margins normalizing  
• Strategic upside: China OEM localisation; Europe/US recovery  

• Key risks: geopolitical cost pressure; export demand slowdown  

• Shareholding: Promoters **65.7%**; FII **4.73%**; DII **21.32%**  
• Financials (CY26E): Revenue **Rs 10,535 cr**; Adj. PAT **Rs 1,088 cr**

Vidya Wires — Ventura Initiating Coverage

**Buy thesis: growth, deleveraging, rerating**

• Rating: **BUY** at CMP ~INR **74**  
• 24‑month TP: **INR 99** (~**35%** upside)  
• FY25–28 revenue CAGR: **~30.5%**  
• FY28E revenue: **~INR 3,303–3,380 cr**  
• FY28E PAT: **~INR 104 cr**  
• RoIC FY28E: **~34%** (high capital efficiency)  
• PEG: **~0.6x** — attractive growth‑valuation mix  
• Capacity doubling to **37,680 MTPA** by FY27  
• Promoter holding **~72.8%**, no pledge  

**Key catalysts / triggers**

• ALCU plant successful commissioning and ramp  
• Strong power & transmission capex recovery  
• Renewable and data‑center demand acceleration  
• Continued margin improvement and EBITDA/ton gains  
• Rapid net‑debt paydown to near **zero** by FY28  

**Primary risks / downside**

• Copper price spikes impair margins materially  
• Supplier concentration (top vendors ~**60%+**)  
• ALCU execution delays or cost overruns  
• High end‑market concentration: Power ~**48%**  
• Historically tight working capital; cash‑flow risk  

Investment view: high growth, rapid deleveraging, asymmetric upside.

Emkay on Specialty Chemicals - China trip reaffirms our views on anti-involution

China is increasingly shifting its focus from bulk chemicals to value-added products, which could have meaningful implications for Indian specialty chemical players.

The potential impact spans companies such as SRF, Navin Fluorine, Gujarat Fluorochemicals GFL, PI Industries, Deepak Nitrite, Aarti Industries, Atul, Jubilant Ingrevia, Laxmi Organic Industries, Chemplast Sanmar, UPL, Coromandel International, and Rallis India.

**PAYTM – RBI ACTION & BROKERAGE VIEW ⚖️📉**

**RATING & TARGET**  
• Rating: Outperform  
• Target Price: ₹1,500  
• CMP: ₹1,147 → ~31% upside  

**KEY EVENT**  
• RBI cancels Paytm Payments Bank (PPBL) license (effective Apr 24, 2026)  
• PPBL to be wound up → no further banking operations  

**REGULATORY BACKDROP**  
• Ban on new customers since Mar 2022  
• Restrictions on deposits & wallet top-ups (Jan–Feb 2024)  
• Issues flagged: governance, compliance, depositor interest  

**IMPACT ASSESSMENT**  

**NEGATIVE (SENTIMENTAL)**  
• Strong regulatory language remains a concern  
• Incremental negative from perception standpoint  

**NEUTRAL (FINANCIALS)**  
• PPBL already non-operational for ~1 year  
• Business separation already executed  
• Write-offs taken earlier → no fresh hit  

**POTENTIAL UPSIDE (OPTIONALITY)**  
• Scope to apply for:  
  • NBFC license  
  • PPI license  
• Enables:  
  • Wallet expansion  
  • Credit product scaling  

**FINANCIAL TRAJECTORY**  
• EPS: FY25 (₹-10.35) → FY26E ₹9.01 → FY27E ₹22.83  
• Revenue/share: steady growth trajectory  

**VALUATION SNAPSHOT**  
• P/E: 127x (FY26E) → 50x (FY27E)  
• High valuation but backed by earnings inflection  

**STOCK PERFORMANCE**  
• YTD: -11.7%  
• 1Y: +26.7%  
• Underperformance vs benchmark persists  

**CORE INSIGHT**  
• RBI action largely “known negative” → limited incremental financial impact  
• Business now cleaner, with regulatory overhang reducing over time
